summary:
Preclinical testing excludes good clinical practice since no human participants are involved; other trial stages strictly adhere to GCP guidelines ensuring participant protection and accurate results. This divergence makes phase zero or preclinical stage unique among those listed regarding compliance with GCP principles. Given this distinction, options B-D can be ruled out easily based solely upon their inclusion within traditional therapeutic development frameworks where rigorous ethical oversight applies consistently across different patient populations studied therein. Hence, maintaining consistency between previous analysis aligns well here too supporting selection 'A'. 

The summary captures essential elements about why only certain parts might deviate concerning application versus others which uniformly apply stringent quality control measures throughout entire process including post-marketing surveillance activities conducted under regulatory scrutiny aimed at safeguarding public interest vis-a-vis pharmaceutical products introduced into medical care systems globally today.
